programme.title: Wild Foods
programme.desc: Members of the Cree Nation reintroduce the American buffalo; a new breed of cattle ranchers on Montana's high plains; conservationists in the Centennial Valley work to restore the Arctic grayling.

programme.title: Samantha Brown's Places to Love
programme.desc: The tiny railroad town of Goffs in the eastern Mojave Desert; Amboy; the only piece of Route 66 that runs through a military installation; Elmer's Bottle Tree Ranch.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: An Andrew Clemens sand bottle circa 1885; a 1933 Goudey baseball card set 239/240; a Joan Miró pencil on paper circa 1965.

programme.title: The American Revolution
programme.sub-title: The Times That Try Men's Souls
programme.desc: British General Howe defeats General Washington, forcing the Americans to abandon New York; Washington's army regroups in New Jersey before attacking an outpost at Trenton.

programme.title: Roadtrip Nation: The Inside Scholars
programme.desc: Three formerly incarcerated folks explore higher education outside of prison.

programme.title: Roadtrip Nation: The Inside Scholars
programme.desc: The classroom inside prison where educators equip scholars with the resources to define their own paths; the roadtrippers reflect on their own journeys and learn what it takes to set themselves free.

programme.title: Amanpour and Company
programme.desc: Christiane Amanpour leads discussions about world issues and interviews with global leaders.

programme.title: 50 Years With Peter, Paul and Mary
programme.desc: Performance footage of folk group Peter, Paul and Mary.

programme.title: This Land Is Your Land (My Music Presents)
programme.desc: The Smothers Brothers and Judy Collins host a look at the evolution of modern American folk music; performers include Glenn Yarborough, the Highwaymen, the Kingston Trio and the Limeliters.

programme.title: Rick Steves Mighty Alps
programme.desc: Rick Steves takes a European alpine adventure from Italy to Austria and Switzerland to France; locations include the Dolomites, Zugspitze, Matterhorn, Aiguille du Midi and the Tour du Mont Blanc hiking path.

programme.title: Lyla in the Loop
programme.sub-title: Lyla Gets a Grip; Rappin' Riddles
programme.desc: Lyla overcomes her fear of rock-climbing; a riddle game about everyday objects made by Black inventors.

programme.title: Wild Kratts
programme.desc: A power suit malfunction turns Martin into an oak tree.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Arthur
programme.sub-title: Carl's Concerto; Too Much of a Good Thing
programme.desc: Carl agrees to play the accordion for George's puppet show; Binky tries to keep Buster from eating too many cookies.

programme.title: Weather Hunters
programme.desc: Lily and her family discover a surprise and also embark on a weather hunting expedition.

programme.title: Lyla in the Loop
programme.desc: Lyla and a new friend build a spaceship; Lyla learns her sisters can predict what the other wants, so she tries to do the same with Luke.

programme.title: Carl the Collector
programme.sub-title: The Sweater Collection; New Bus Stop
programme.desc: Carl loves the new sweater his Pop Pop knit him, but it feels itchy when he wears it; Carl is nervous about his new school bus stop.

programme.title: Daniel Tiger's Neighborhood
programme.sub-title: Daniel Does Gymnastics; The Big Slide
programme.desc: Daniel is nervous about going to gymnastics; Daniel and O the Owl are excited to ride the big slide.

programme.title: Phoebe & Jay
programme.desc: Phoebe learns what privacy signs are and how to use them to communicate to neighbors; Jay gets nervous about getting a haircut.

programme.title: Sesame Street
programme.desc: Rudy is afraid of getting a shot at the doctor's office, so Nina and Abby play pretend to show what the visit will be like so he can feel less scared.

programme.title: Work It Out Wombats!
programme.sub-title: Survey Says; Lost and Found Patrol
programme.desc: Mr. E has a lot of ping-pong balls and the survey says that he should sell the purple ones; Super's lost her glasses and the Lost and Found Patrol try to find them.

programme.title: Donkey Hodie
programme.sub-title: Quick Quack Pack; Find a Way to Fix It
programme.desc: Duck wants to fly with the bigger birds; Donkey loses Bob Dog's favorite toy.

programme.title: Rosie's Rules
programme.desc: Rosie wants Javi's popcorn for family movie night, so she tries to find the perfect trade; Rosie makes a seashell museum at the beach.

programme.title: Elinor Wonders Why
programme.sub-title: Melting Point; Mr. Raccoon's Bad Day
programme.desc: While delivering chocolates, the kids learn that warmer temperatures can melt treats; the kids help Mr. Raccoon calm down when he is having a bad day.

programme.title: Alma's Way
programme.sub-title: Do the Waltzango; Big Brother Bootcamp
programme.desc: Eddie and Becka create a new mash-up dance; Alma teaches Eddie how to be a great big brother.

programme.title: Molly of Denali
programme.desc: Molly learns how to make offerings, hoping to stop a volcano from erputing.

programme.sub-title: I Am Mary Leakey; I Am Alexander Hamilton

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: An Andrew Clemens sand bottle circa 1885; a 1933 Goudey baseball card set 239/240; a Joan Miró pencil on paper circa 1965.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Comics Tim Vine and Ricky Grover search for profitable antiques in southeast Scotland; David Harper and James Braxton guide them; the teams gamble on a mahogany chair and pewter candlesticks.

programme.title: The Forsytes on Masterpiece
programme.desc: Soames commissions a country house despite Irene's distress; as Louisa faces a fresh start, Jo decides his future; Ann reveals a dark secret.

programme.title: BBC News The Context
programme.desc: A regular panel of guests from across the political spectrum discuss the biggest international stories of the day.

programme.title: BBC News America
programme.desc: Live news program produced out of BBC's Washington, D.C. bureau; host Sumi Somaskanda.

programme.title: PBS News Hour
programme.desc: Co-anchors Amna Nawaz and Geoff Bennett and correspondents offer in-depth analysis of current events.

programme.title: In the Americas With David Yetman
programme.sub-title: Imperiled Refuges: Islets in the Sea of Cortes
programme.desc: Islands and waters of Mexico's northern Gulf of California provide refuge for sea creatures reliant on fish stocks and beset with the uncertainties of climate change.

programme.title: Outside: Beyond the Lens
programme.desc: Jeff, Zack and Dave, along with local guides, discover the history and culture of Hvar's past.

programme.title: Finding Your Roots With Henry Louis Gates, Jr.
programme.desc: Rebecca Hall and Lee Daniels research their family histories.

programme.title: The American Revolution
programme.desc: General Howe takes Philadelphia, but the Americans' surprising victory over General Burgoyne at Saratoga encourages France to support the U.S.

programme.title: Lafayette: The Lost Hero
programme.desc: Marquis de Lafayette left France at the age of 19 and helped fight for America's freedom.

programme.title: Amanpour and Company
programme.desc: Christiane Amanpour leads discussions about world issues and interviews with global leaders.

programme.title: America's Home Cooking: Flavors of the Fourth
programme.desc: Celebrating picnics and barbecue with Chris Fennimore and the America's Home Cooking team; recipes include horseradish potato salad, baked beans, apple hand pies and more summer favorites.

programme.title: Rick Steves' Europe: Great German Cities
programme.desc: Rick Steves explores the German cities of Hamburg, Dresden, Leipzig, Frankfurt and Nuremberg, including their Baroque palaces, skyscrapers, riversides and beer halls.

programme.title: Lyla in the Loop
programme.sub-title: The Artist Formerly Known as Liana; Jamaica Day
programme.desc: Liana accidentally submits art that Stu made and feels guilty taking the credit for his work; Lyla and Luke perform an "Anansi" folktale puppet show.

programme.title: Wild Kratts
programme.desc: The Kratt Bros. discover there is an old wily orca who only eats large sharks; they find this hard to believe, so with the help of Aviva's dolphin decoder, modified to speak orca, the Wild Kratts go in search of the orca who likes sharks.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Arthur's not sure what to make of the new baby; D.W. considers running away to Button Island.

programme.desc: The Hunters find a stowaway in their picnic basket, a little furry dog.

programme.title: Work It Out Wombats!

programme.desc: Donkey Hodie and pals help Purple Panda celebrate his first Halloween in Someplace Else.

programme.title: Finding Your Roots With Henry Louis Gates, Jr.
programme.desc: Rebecca Hall and Lee Daniels research their family histories.

programme.title: Our Vanishing Americana: Florida
programme.desc: Our Vanishing Florida Americana showcases photographer Mike Lassiter's quest to capture the essence of Florida's back roads, coastlines, and main streets.

programme.title: MacGillivray Freeman's Coral Reef Adventure
programme.desc: Filmmakers Howard and Michele Hall embark on a daring expedition across the South Pacific to document the world's most beautiful and endangered coral reefs.

programme.desc: A regular panel of guests from across the political spectrum discuss the biggest international stories of the day.

programme.title: PBS News Hour

programme.title: Energy Switch
programme.desc: Politicians from different parties collaborate on climate and energy challenges; with Kay Bailey Hutchison and Joe Manchin.

programme.title: American Grown: My Job Depends on Ag
programme.desc: The future of the agriculture industry and stories of how it touches us all far beyond the table.

programme.title: Nature
programme.desc: Exploring the unique evolutionary journey of woodpeckers and the role they play in every ecosystem, except Antarctica and Australia.
